title: Squash Casserole
description: Tender yellow squash baked under a buttery cracker topping. The side dish that disappears first at every family supper.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 350°F and butter a 9x13 baking dish.
Cook the squash and onion in half the butter over medium heat until soft, about 10 minutes, then drain well.
Stir in the eggs, sour cream, 1 cup of the cheese, half the crackers, salt, and pepper.
Spread the mixture in the dish and top with the remaining cheese and crackers.
Melt the rest of the butter and drizzle it over the top.
Bake for 30 to 35 minutes, until golden and bubbly at the edges.
notes: Drain the squash well or the casserole turns watery — press it gently in a colander.
leftovers: Keeps 3 days in the refrigerator and reheats beautifully.
storage: Cover the dish with foil and refrigerate. Reheat at 325°F until warm in the middle.
whyWeChoseThis: Uses up summer squash before it turns.
Fits the weekly budget at about $9.
Reheats beautifully for a second night.
